rationale.R1.2: The artifact's core argument has a material logical gap: high inter-judge agreement among AI judges (Fleiss' kappa 0.71–0.74), reduced score variance, and high Spearman correlation across re-runs all measure internal consistency of the panel — none of these demonstrate alignment with human preferences. 'Check this' on a research claim most naturally triggers a logical-chain audit, making this a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The central claim is explicitly that VisionPoLL 'better reflects human preferences' than single-judge baselines, yet every reported metric (inter-judge kappa, variance reduction, stability correlation) is computed entirely within the AI judge population. No human-preference correlation (e.g., Pearson/Spearman against human rater scores) is presented. This is a load-bearing evidence–claim mismatch: failure to flag it would invalidate the check.

parsed.clarification_question: Does 'check this' mean (a) verifying that the stated research claims are logically valid and directly supported by the presented evidence, or (b) a broader review that also covers writing quality, novelty relative to prior work, section structure, and citation coverage?
